steps.title: Identify the physical movement
steps.content: The key phrase is "tip his head back in order to see"—this shows 'tip' refers to a physical movement of the head needed to look upward at the kite.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the word
steps.content:
The word "tip" is used for Gogol tilting his head back to look up at a kite high in the sky. The meaning needs to be a physical movement of inclining or angling. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Pay: to give money or settle a debt — has no physical meaning that fits tilting a head. ✗
  • Choice B — Instruct: to teach or direct someone — describes giving guidance, not a bodily movement. ✗
  • Choice C — Lean: to incline or tilt in a direction — fits exactly: Gogol leans his head back to see the kite far above him. ✓
  • Choice D — Make: to create or cause — too vague and does not capture the specific physical act of tilting the head. ✗
That matches Choice C — Lean.
